description_long: This object is a vintage radio receiver with a rectangular wooden enclosure and a black front control panel. The front panel features numerous metallic knobs, switches, and buttons, as well as a horizontal frequency display window with a tuning indicator. The top surface has a rectangular black mesh or grille, likely for ventilation or internal speaker output. The rear panel contains various input/output ports, labels, and connection terminals for audio and power. The sides and top are finished in a wood veneer, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The overall design suggests it was intended for tabletop use in a home or office environment.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a black control panel, multiple knobs, and a frequency display window. The top has a black mesh grille, and the back features various connection ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the black control panel with many knobs, switches, and a horizontal frequency display window.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood veneer side panel and the edge of the front control panel.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing multiple input/output ports, labels, and connection terminals on a black panel.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain wood veneer side panel and a partial view of the front controls.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
